title: carex bigelowii subsp. paishanensis
intro: Carex bigelowii subsp. paishanensis, also known as Paishan sedge, is a type of perennial plant belonging to the family Cyperaceae, native to mountainous regions of Taiwan. Traditionally, Paishan sedge has been used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions due to its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The plant has been found to have several health benefits, including reducing fever, alleviating pain, and promoting wound healing. Paishan sedge can be prepared as a herbal tea or infused in hot water to be used as a medicinal treatment, with some studies suggesting its potential use in modern medicine for its antimicrobial and antioxidant properties.
